# agentic-misp-mcp

## Summary

agentic-misp-mcp is an MCP server for analyst-oriented MISP workflows. It is workflow-first, not
endpoint-first. It exposes bounded tools for IOC search, investigation, pivoting, event
summarization, report generation, proposal generation, and tightly controlled approval-gated
writes.

It must not become a raw MISP API proxy.

## Project status

- Early development.
- `main` contains `v0.2.0-beta.2` (config doctor, approval-store pruning, rollback playbook,
  closed test gaps — no new MCP tools, no new MISP write capability), merged from
  `hardening/v0.2.0-beta.2-operational-readiness`. This is `v0.2.0-rc.1`, a **release candidate
  for GA review**, built on top of `v0.2.0-beta.2`: it adds `propose_event`/`propose_attribute`
  payload validation, a MISP version compatibility matrix (`docs/misp-compatibility.md`), and a
  fixed Dependabot config. Not GA production-ready. Production deployment is not yet validated;
  see production-readiness.md and ga-production-readiness-plan.md for scope, requirements, and
  acceptance criteria.
- Current MCP tool count: 19 (unchanged since Phase 8; config doctor/approvals prune/the new
  validation layer are not new MCP tools).
- Primary transport: stdio.
- HTTP transport is experimental.
- Python 3.11+.
- License: MIT.
- Automated tests use mocked MISP responses, plus live-lab validation (see below).
- Live read-only lab validation passed against MISP 2.5.42 using Docker, stdio, and MCP Inspector.
- Core controlled-write validation passed in the same lab: submit_ioc_with_approval, add_sighting_with_approval, tag_event_with_approval, publish_event_with_approval, plus policy blocking. Two real bugs found during that pass are fixed (see "Live validation notes" below).
- Still pending (live evidence, not mocked): large event/result-set behavior at realistic scale,
  rate-limit/timeout/TLS failure modes, warninglist endpoint compatibility, broader MISP version
  compatibility, and final sign-off. `v0.2.0-beta.2` closed the large-result/rate-limit/warninglist-hit/warninglist-not_available gaps with mocked/controlled tests only (see `tests/test_operational_gap_closure.py` and `docs/live-validation-report-v0.2.0-beta.2.md`). `v0.2.0-rc.1` closes propose_event/propose_attribute payload-shape validation at the code/test level only (`tests/test_proposal_validation.py`); live cross-checking against a real MISP instance remains open.

## v0.2.0-beta.2 operational-readiness hardening

- `agentic-misp-mcp config doctor` — PASS/WARN/FAIL operational-readiness checks beyond
  `config-check`: write/approval-mode pairing, publish/role pairing, approval-store and audit-log
  permission safety, production write allowlist coverage, approval TTL length, temp paths, and
  leftover lab tokens in production mode. Never connects to MISP, never prints secrets, exits
  nonzero on any FAIL.
- `agentic-misp-mcp approvals prune --older-than <duration> [--vacuum]` — operator-CLI-only;
  deletes old terminal (used/rejected/expired) approval records past an age threshold
  (`7d`/`30d`/`24h`/`3600s`-style durations); never deletes pending/approved records; not exposed
  through any MCP tool.
- `docs/rollback.md` — rollback playbook for a mistaken controlled write.

## v0.2.0-rc.1 GA-readiness release candidate

Builds on `v0.2.0-beta.2`. A release candidate for GA review, not a GA claim. No new MCP tools,
no new MISP write capability, no raw proxy/admin behavior.

- `propose_event`/`propose_attribute` validate the proposed payload before building it: required
  fields, `distribution`/`threat_level_id`/`analysis` ranges, tag list shape, and a
  known-vocabulary allowlist of standard MISP attribute types/categories
  (`src/agentic_misp_mcp/policy/proposal_validation.py`). Malformed/unsupported payloads return
  `status: "invalid"` (audited as `outcome: "invalid"`) with `validation_errors`, never a
  proposal; MISP is never contacted either way.
- Fixed `.github/dependabot.yml` (previously blank `package-ecosystem`, so no updates were
  actually running); now tracks `pip` and `github-actions`.
- Added `docs/misp-compatibility.md` (MISP version compatibility matrix) and
  `docs/live-beta-validation-v0.2.0-rc.1.md` (this release candidate's live validation checklist).
- Still open: live edge-case evidence (TLS fail-closed, timeout, real HTTP 429, large-result
  truncation at scale, a positive warninglist hit against real data), live cross-checking of
  propose_event/propose_attribute payload shapes against a real MISP instance, broader MISP
  version compatibility, and supply-chain/release hygiene (container/dependency/secret scanning,
  a signed release tag) — see `docs/ga-production-readiness-plan.md`.

## Safety boundaries

Agents and contributors must preserve these boundaries:

- Do not expose raw MISP API passthrough tools.
- Do not add generic admin tools for users, organisations, servers, settings, or unrestricted MISP endpoints.
- Do not pass API keys, approval tokens, authorization headers, passwords, cookies, or secrets through MCP tool arguments.
- Do not add shell execution or unrestricted filesystem access.
- Keep read-only mode as the safe default.
- Keep controlled writes disabled by default with AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ENABLE_WRITE=false.
- Keep write approval required by default with AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=true.
- Preserve audit logging for every MCP tool call.
- Preserve argument sanitization and secret redaction.
- Preserve bounded output and response-size limits.

## Important environment variables

- MISP_URL: required MISP base URL.
- MISP_API_KEY: required runtime API key; never pass as a tool argument.
- MISP_VERIFY_TLS: default true; false is lab-only for self-signed certificates.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ROLE: read_only, analyst_write, curator, or admin.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ENABLE_WRITE: default false.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_REQUIRE_APPROVAL: default true.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_APPROVAL_TOKEN: optional lab/shared-secret hardening only; audit logs redact it. It is not the production approval mechanism.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_AUDIT_LOG_PATH: JSONL audit path.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES: maximum MISP response size before JSON parsing.

## Tool groups

Read-only investigation:

- search_ioc
- investigate_ioc
- summarize_event
- check_warninglists

Pivoting and event intelligence:

- pivot_ioc
- find_related_iocs
- extract_event_iocs
- explain_event_context
- find_events_by_tag

Reporting:

- generate_ioc_report
- generate_event_report
- generate_markdown_ioc_report
- generate_markdown_event_report

Proposal-only tools:

- propose_event
- propose_attribute

Approval-gated write tools:

- submit_ioc_with_approval
- add_sighting_with_approval
- tag_event_with_approval
- publish_event_with_approval

Write tools must return explicit outcomes such as blocked, pending_approval, executed, or failed.
"failed" means MISP was actually reached and did not raise an error, but MISP itself rejected the
operation (for example an HTTP 200 response with saved/published: false) — distinct from
"executed", which means MISP confirmed the write.

## Audit semantics

Audit logs are JSONL.

Expected audit outcomes:

- Allowed successful call: success=true, outcome=success.
- Blocked policy decision: allowed=false, success=false, outcome=blocked.
- Runtime error or validation failure: success=false, outcome=error.
- Controlled write reached MISP but MISP rejected the operation (tool status="failed"):
  success=false, outcome=failed. Distinct from both blocked and error.

Sensitive values such as approval_token, API keys, authorization headers, bearer tokens, cookies,
passwords, and secrets must be redacted.

## Docker usage

Build:

```bash
cd /path/to/agentic-misp-mcp
docker build -t agentic-misp-mcp:live-test .
```

Run config-check:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  --env-file /home/user/.config/agentic-misp-mcp/live.env \
  -v /home/user/.local/state/agentic-misp-mcp/logs:/app/logs \
  agentic-misp-mcp:live-test \
  config-check
```

Run stdio server:

```bash
docker run --rm -i \
  --env-file /home/user/.config/agentic-misp-mcp/live.env \
  -v /home/user/.local/state/agentic-misp-mcp/logs:/app/logs \
  agentic-misp-mcp:live-test \
  --transport stdio
```

Run with MCP Inspector:

```bash
npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector \
  docker run --rm -i \
    --env-file /home/user/.config/agentic-misp-mcp/live.env \
    -v /home/user/.local/state/agentic-misp-mcp/logs:/app/logs \
    agentic-misp-mcp:live-test \
    --transport stdio
```

For headless hosts, forward Inspector ports:

```bash
ssh -L 6274:127.0.0.1:6274 -L 6277:127.0.0.1:6277 user@mcp-host.example.local
```

## Development commands

Use these commands before committing code changes:

```bash
uv run --extra dev ruff check .
uv run --extra dev ruff format --check .
uv run --extra dev pytest -q
```

Equivalent Make targets:

```bash
make lint
make format-check
make test
make check
```

## Live validation notes

Read-only and core controlled-write live validation both passed against MISP 2.5.42.

Validated:

- Docker runtime.
- config-check.
- MISP /servers/getVersion.
- MCP Inspector stdio connection, both browser mode and headless --cli mode.
- tools/list.
- search_ioc.
- investigate_ioc.
- summarize_event.
- generate_ioc_report.
- check_warninglists.
- find_events_by_tag.
- composite domain|ip matching from both IP and domain sides.
- SHA256 lookup.
- audit logging for success, validation failure, blocked write attempts, and MISP-side write
  rejections (outcome=failed).
- read-only write blocking with MISP unchanged after attempted write.
- error paths: unreachable MISP_URL and invalid MISP_API_KEY both fail cleanly with no crash.
- submit_ioc_with_approval, add_sighting_with_approval, tag_event_with_approval, and
  publish_event_with_approval, including role-based publish blocking (analyst_write cannot
  publish), tested against a dedicated sandbox event.

Two real bugs were found and fixed during controlled-write validation:

- A blank/whitespace AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_APPROVAL_TOKEN was treated as a configured token rather
  than "unset," silently blocking all controlled writes. Now normalizes to unset.
- tag_event_with_approval/publish_event_with_approval reported status="executed" even when MISP
  itself rejected the operation (HTTP 200 with saved/published: false). Both now report a
  distinct status="failed" with a matching outcome="failed" audit entry.

Still pending:

- propose_event/propose_attribute payload validation against a real MISP instance.
- pivot edge cases.
- warninglist edge cases and endpoint compatibility across MISP versions.
- large event/result-set behavior.
- rate-limit/timeout/TLS failure modes.
- broader MISP version compatibility beyond 2.5.42.
- final sign-off (docs/live-validation-plan.md section 9).

This is lab validation evidence, not a production certification.

## v0.2.0-beta.2 live validation notes

`config doctor` and `approvals prune` were both run against the same MISP 2.5.42 lab used above,
plus a read-only regression smoke test (check_warninglists, search_attributes) confirming
beta.2's additive-only CLI changes did not regress the existing read-only path. No bugs found.
See docs/live-validation-report-v0.2.0-beta.2.md for full evidence. The HTTP 429/large-result/
warninglist-hit/warninglist-not_available gaps listed as "still pending" above were closed with
mocked/controlled tests only in beta.2 (tests/test_operational_gap_closure.py), not with live
reproduction — reproducing a real 429 or an oversized live MISP result was treated as out of
scope (no DoS-style testing).

## Known limitation

The first positive live validation used historical OSINT data from 2016. Treat it as
compatibility and workflow evidence, not current threat activity. Analyst workflows should
correlate with current local telemetry before blocking or escalation.

## Instructions for coding agents

When editing this repository:

- Keep the server workflow-oriented.
- Preserve read-only default behavior.
- Preserve policy gates.
- Preserve approval gates.
- Preserve audit logging and redaction.
- Add or update tests for any behavior change.
- Do not weaken role checks.
- Do not add raw MISP proxy functionality.
- Do not include secrets or private lab details in docs or tests.
- Prefer generic examples in documentation.
- Show diffs before committing.
- Do not claim production readiness anywhere in docs. See production-readiness.md for the
  current scope and acceptance criteria before making any production-status claim.


## v0.2.0-beta.1 production-write beta

Production approval mode is opt-in via `AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_APPROVAL_MODE=production`. It applies only to the four existing write-executing approval tools and requires `approval_request_id` redemption against SQLite state approved by the operator CLI. Do not add MCP approval/admin tools. Do not add new MISP write capabilities. `approved=true` alone must not execute in production mode. Lab mode remains the default.


## Production approval mode (`v0.2.0-beta.1`)

- Lab mode (`AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_APPROVAL_MODE=lab`) is the default. `approved=true` is a lab programmatic gate; `AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_APPROVAL_TOKEN` is only optional lab/shared-secret hardening.
- Production mode requires `AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_APPROVAL_MODE=production`; `approved=true` alone never executes writes, even if `AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false`.
- Production writes require an operator-approved `approval_request_id` created in SQLite and approved via `agentic-misp-mcp approvals ...`; approvals are one-time-use, TTL-bound, and exact operation-hash-bound.
- The LLM/agent must not have shell access to the approval CLI or write access to the approval database. If redemption succeeds but MISP later fails, the approval is consumed and retry needs a new operator approval.
- Guardrails include `AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTE_TYPES`, `AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTE_CATEGORIES`, `AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ALLOWED_TAGS`, and `AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ENABLE_PUBLISH`.
